title: androsaemum × parviflorum
intro: Androsaemum × parviflorum, commonly known as Rock Cress or Creeping Thyme, is a flowering plant in the genus Androsaemum, characterized by its tiny, white, pink, or purple flowers and its spreading habit. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may aid in soothing skin irritations and wounds. Consuming Rock Cress is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs and colds, and possibly lowering blood sugar levels. Rock Cress can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
